commit -m:
Specify a descriptive message for submission. Explain the changes made during this submission.
eg. git commit -m "Add new feature"
commit -a: Automatically add tracked file changes to the staging area and submit them.
commit --amend: Modify the most recently submitted content or submission message.
rebase: ✨forward-port local commits to the updated upstream head
init: ✨create empty git repository or re-initialize an existing one
init --bare:
Initialize a bare repository
don't create .git folder, but instead place all its contents directly in the current directory
clone --depth: ✨Specifies the depth of commit history to clone
clone --depth 1: Only the most recent commit is cloned, there is no history
push: update remote refs along with associated objects
pull: fetch from and merge with another repository or local branch
branch -a: List all branches, including local and remote branches.
branch -d:
Delete the specified branch (use -D to force delete it)
eg. git branch -d <branch>
branch -m:
Rename the specified branch (use -M to force rename it)
eg. git branch -m <old-branch-name> <new-branch-name>
checkout:
✨checkout branch or paths to working tree.
eg. git checkout <branch-name>  --- Switch Branch
    git checkout -b <new-branch-name>  --- Create and switch branches
    git checkout <commit-hash>  --- Switch to specific submission
checkout -b:
Create and switch branches
eg. git checkout -b <new-branch-name>
merge: ✨join two or more development histories together
fetch: download objects and refs from another repository
diff: ✨show changes between commits, commits and working tree, etc.
reflog show: Show the commit history in the reflog
reflog expire: Expire reflog entries to remove old entries
remote set-head: Set the default branch on a remote repository
remote set-branches: Set the list of branches to track on a remote repository
remote get-url: Display the URL for a remote repository
remote set-url: Change the URL of a remote repository
remote show: Show information about a remote repository
remote prune: Prune stale references from a remote repository
remote update: Fetch updates from a remote repository
tag: create, list, delete or verify tag object signed with GPG
config: get and set repository or global options
stash: ✨Stash away changes to the dirty working directory
stash push: Push changes onto the stash stack
stash save: Save changes on the stash stack
stash show: Show the changes in a stash
stash pop: Apply and remove changes from a stash
stash create: Create a new stash with changes
stash branch: Create a new branch from a stash
cherry-pick: ✨apply changes introduced by some existing commits
apply: apply patch to files and/or to index
archimport: import an Arch repository into git
archive: create archive of files from named tree
bisect: ✨find, by binary search, change that introduced a bug
bisect bad: mark current or given revision as bad
bisect good: mark current or given revision as good
bisect log: show log of the current bisection
bisect next: find next bisection to test and check it out
bisect new: mark current or given revision as new, indicating a bad state
bisect old: mark current or given revision as old, indicating a good state
bisect reset: finish bisection search and return to the given branch
bisect start: reset bisection state and start a new bisection
bisect terms: show the terms used for old and new commits (default: bad, good)
bisect terms --term-good: change the terms used for old and new commits (default: good)
bisect terms --term-bad: change the terms used for old and new commits (default: bad)
bisect view: show the remaining revisions in gitk
bisect visualize: show the remaining revisions in gitk
blame: show what revision and author last modified each line of a file
bugreport: ✨collect information for user to file a bug report
bugreport -o:
--output-directory,-o
specify a destination for the bugreport file
bugreport --output-directory:
--output-directory,-o
specify a destination for the bugreport file
bugreport -s:
--suffix,-s
specify a strftime format suffix for the filename
bugreport --suffix:
--suffix,-s
specify a strftime format suffix for the filename
bundle verify: check that a bundle is valid and will apply cleanly
cat-file: provide content or type information for repository objects
check-mailmap: ✨show canonical names and email addresses of contacts
checkout-index: copy files from index to working directory
check-ref-format: ensure that a reference name is well formed
clean: remove untracked files from working tree
commit-graph: write and verify Git commit-graph files
count-objects: ✨count unpacked objects and display their disk consumption
count-objects -H: print sizes in human readable format
count-objects --human-readable: print sizes in human readable format
count-objects -v: also report number of in-pack objects and objects that can be removed
count-objects --verbose: also report number of in-pack objects and objects that can be removed
daemon: run a really simple server for git repositories
describe: show most recent tag that is reachable from a commit
diff-files: compare files in working tree and index
diff-index: compare content and mode of blobs between index and repository
diff-tree: compare content and mode of blobs found via two tree objects
flow init: Initialize a new git repo with support for the branching model
flow log: Show log deviating from base branch
fetch-pack: receive missing objects from another repository
for-each-repo: run a git command on a list of repositories
fsck: verify connectivity and validity of objects in database
gc: cleanup unnecessary files and optimize local repository
get-tar-commit-id: extract commit ID from an archive created using git archive
gui: run portable graphical interface to git
hash-object: compute object ID and optionally create a blob from a file
http-backend: run a server side implementation of Git over HTTP
http-fetch: download from remote git repository via HTTP
http-push: push objects over HTTP/DAV to another repository
imap-send: send a collection of patches to an IMAP folder
index-pack: build pack index file for an existing packed archive
instaweb: instantly browse your working repository in gitweb
interpret-trailers: add or parse structured information in commit messages
ls-files: information about files in index/working directory
ls-remote: show references in a remote repository
ls-tree: list contents of a tree object
mv: move or rename file, directory, or symlink
mailinfo: extract patch and authorship from a single email message
mailsplit: split mbox file into a list of files
maintenance: run tasks to optimize Git repository data
merge-base: find as good a common ancestor as possible for a merge
merge-index: run merge for files needing merging
merge-one-file: standard helper-program to use with git merge-index
mergetool: ✨run merge conflict resolution tools to resolve merge conflicts
merge-tree: show three-way merge without touching index
mktag: create tag object with extra validation
mktree: build tree-object from git ls-tree formatted text
notes append: Append notes to an existing object
notes copy: Copy notes from one object to another
notes edit: Edit existing notes for an object
notes get-ref: Get the reference for notes on an object
notes merge: Merge notes from one object into another
notes prune: Prune notes from the object database
name-rev: find symbolic names for given revisions
pack-refs: pack heads and tags for efficient repository access
parse-remote: routines to help parsing remote repository access parameters
patch-id: compute unique ID for a patch
prune: prune all unreachable objects from the object database
prune-packed: remove extra objects that are already in pack files
rm: remove files from the working tree and from the index
rerere clear: Clear all saved conflict resolution records
rerere forget: Forget and discard specified conflict resolution records
rerere diff: Show differences in conflict resolution records
rerere status: Show the current status of conflict resolution records
rerere gc: Garbage collect unnecessary conflict resolution records
read-tree: read tree information into directory index
receive-pack: receive what is pushed into repository
repack: pack unpacked objects in a repository
replace: create, list, delete refs to replace objects
rev-list: list commit object in reverse chronological order
rev-parse: pick out and message parameters for other git commands
send-email: send collection of patches as emails
send-pack: push objects over git protocol to another repository
shell: restricted login shell for GIT-only SSH access
show-ref: list references in a local repository
submodule add: Add a new submodule to the current project
submodule status: Show the status of submodules in the current project
submodule init: Initialize submodules for the current project
submodule deinit: Deinitialize submodules for the current project
submodule update: Update submodules in the current project
submodule summary: Show a summary of submodule status
submodule foreach: Run a command in each submodule
subtree: split repository into subtree and merge them
svn: ✨bidirectional operation between a subversion repository and git
svn init: Initialize a Git repository for Subversion (SVN) integration
svn clone: Clone a Subversion (SVN) repository into Git
svn rebase: Rebase the current branch onto SVN commit
svn dcommit: Commit Git changes to Subversion (SVN) repository
svn find-rev: Find a Subversion (SVN) revision corresponding to a Git commit
svn set-tree: Set the current Git tree to be equivalent to an SVN checkout
svn commit-diff: Commit the changes in a Git diff to Subversion (SVN)
svn info: Display information about the Subversion (SVN) repository
svn create-ignore: Create an ignore file from Subversion (SVN) properties
svn propget: Get a Subversion (SVN) property from the repository
svn show-ignore: Show the ignore patterns from Subversion (SVN) properties
svn branch: Create a new branch in the Subversion (SVN) repository
svn tag: Create a new tag in the Subversion (SVN) repository
svn blame: Show blame annotations from Subversion (SVN) repository
svn migrate: Migrate an SVN repository to Git
svn reset: Reset the current branch to the last fetched state
svn gc: Perform housekeeping on the Git-SVN repository
unpack-file: create temporary file with blob's contents
update-index: register file contents in the working directory to the index
update-ref: update object name stored in a reference safely
update-server-info: update auxiliary information file to help dumb servers
upload-pack: send objects packed back to git fetch-pack
whatchanged: show commit-logs and differences they introduce
worktree: manage multiple working dirs attached to the same repository
write-tree: ✨Create a tree object from the current index
write-tree add: Add entries to the index and create a tree object
write-tree list: List the entries in the index
write-tree lock: Lock the index to prevent concurrent access
write-tree move: Move or rename an entry in the index
write-tree prune: Prune empty directories from the index
write-tree unlock: Unlock the index to allow concurrent access
